q: The branch of physics and technology concerned with the design of circuits using transistors, integrated circuits and microchips is known as ___.
Active electrical components require a ___ to operate.
___ electrical components are capable of operating without an external power source.
Zener diode is a heavily doped semiconductor pn ___ diode.
A zener diode makes the current flow in the ___ direction when reverse biased.
Zener diode has a turn-on voltage of between ___ in the forward-bias mode.
When the reverse voltage increases to the breakdown voltage, the junction breaks down and large current starts flowing; this effect is known as the ___.
___ occurs at junctions which being lightly doped have wide depletion layers.
___ occurs at junctions which being heavily doped have narrow depletion layers.
For normal operation of a zener diode in breakdown region, the current through the diode should be limited by an ___.
The voltage drop across the zener diode always remains ___ irrespective of the applied voltage.
A ___ is a semiconductor light source that emits light when current flows through it.
LEDs are made from exotic semiconductor compounds such as ___.
In LED the energy is radiated in the form of ___.
LEDs do not directly produce ___ light.
A blue LED with a ___ coating can convert blue light to white light by a process called fluorescence.
A ___ resistor should be connected in series with LED in the circuit to protect the LED.
LEDs have fast action and no warmup time, with a response time of about ___.
The LED device is known to have a much wider ___ compared to the laser.
A ___ is a semiconductor device that converts light into electrical current.
Photodiodes work in ___ biased mode.
When the photodiode is illuminated with light of energy $h \nu$ greater than the energy gap $E_g$ of the semiconductor, then ___ are generated.
A small amount of current, called ___, due to the flow of minority carriers, is produced when no light is falling on the photodiode.
___ is an electric current produced by electromagnetic radiation in the photoelectric effect, photovoltaic effect or photoconductivity.
A ___ is a light controlled variable resistor.
In the dark, the resistance of an LDR is quite high, called ___.
As light level increases, the LDR resistance ___.
Special semiconductor crystal, such as ___ is used to make photo resistors.
A ___ is a temperature sensor that exhibits a large change in resistance proportional to a change in temperature.
The most commonly used thermistor is the ___ thermistor.
The NTC's resistance decreases as the temperature ___, and vice versa.
In the ___ thermistor, the resistance increases as the temperature increases.
___ are a state of matter which has properties between those of conventional liquids and those of solid crystals.
Liquid crystals do not emit light directly, instead using a ___ or reflector to produce images.
A ___ is an optical device that can convert an unpolarized light wave into a polarized light wave by blocking all other vibrations.
___ is a field of electronics involving the study of digital signals and the engineering of devices that use or produce them.
An advantage of digital circuits is that signals can be transmitted without ___ caused by noise.
An ___ is a semiconductor wafer on which thousands or millions of tiny resistors, capacitors and transistors are fabricated.
___ have continuously variable output that depends on the input signal level.
Digital ICs operate at only a few defined levels or states, called ___ (logic 0) and ___ (logic 1).
The fundamental building blocks of digital ICs are ___.
Two common types of package available for logic ICs are known as ___ and ___.
The AND gate with inverted output is called a ___ gate.
The OR gate with inverted output is called a ___ gate.
XOR stands for ___.
In an ___ gate, the output is HIGH if one, and only one, of the inputs is HIGH.
___ gate is an XOR gate whose output is inverted.
___ is a branch of mathematics where variables can have only two possible values: false and true.
The commutative law of addition is expressed as ___.
The commutative law of multiplication is expressed as ___.
The associative law of addition is expressed as ___.
The distributive law is expressed as ___.
According to the basic rules of Boolean algebra, $A + 1 =$ ___.
According to the basic rules of Boolean algebra, $A \cdot 0 =$ ___.
According to the basic rules of Boolean algebra, $A + A =$ ___.
The rule of double inversion in Boolean algebra is $\overline{\overline{A}} =$ ___.
De Morgan's first theorem states that $\overline{A \cdot B} =$ ___.
De Morgan's second theorem states that $\overline{A + B} =$ ___.
In a fire alarm system, the alarm will turn on when either ___ or ___ is detected.
In a street lighting system, the OR gate gives a 1 output when either or both of its inputs are ___.
An electronic communication system is composed of the transmission, reception, and ___ of information.
The basic components of an electronic communication system are the transmitter, communication channel, receiver and ___.
___ is a device that generates information (messages or data) to be transmitted.
The ___ processes the message signal into an appropriate form for transmission and subsequent reception.
___ is the medium that connects transmitter and receiver to convey the information.
___ is an unwanted signal that disturbs, interferes and affects the transmitted signal.
Noise cannot be prevented but can be ___.
The measure of noise is usually expressed in terms of ___.
Digital signals are more ___ to noise than analog signals.
The ___ is to reconstruct the transmitted signal and deliver it to the destination.
In ___ mode, communication takes place over a link between a single transmitter and a receiver.
In the ___ mode, there are a large number of receivers corresponding to a single transmitter.
___ and ___ are examples of broadcast mode of communication.
Analog communication uses an analog signal which varies in ___, phase, or some other property with time.
Data can be broken into ___ as discrete messages in digital communication.
In ___ communication, there is a physical connection between source and destination.
The simplest space communications rely on two things: a ___ and a ___.
A ___ is a device for converting a non-electrical input into an electrical signal (or) vice versa.
The process of converting energy from one form to another is known as ___.
Loudspeakers, microphones, and oximeters are common examples of ___.
The process of changing some characteristics of a carrier wave in accordance with the intensity of the signal is known as ___.
Modulation permits the transmission to occur at ___ while simultaneously allowing the carrying of the audio signal.
There are three basic types of modulation: (i) Amplitude modulation (AM), (ii) Frequency modulation (FM) and (iii) ___.
In amplitude modulation, only the ___ of the carrier wave is changed in accordance with the intensity of the signal.
In frequency modulation, only the ___ of the carrier wave is changed in accordance with the signal.
Phase modulation encodes information as variations in the instantaneous ___ of a carrier wave.
FM radio ranges in a higher spectrum from ___.
AM radio ranges from ___.
The process of recovering the audio signal from the modulated wave is known as ___.
A simple radio receiver comprises antenna, tuner, rf amplifier, ___, af amplifier and speaker.
The receiving antenna picks up the radio signals which is coupled to the ___ circuit through the inductor $L$.
The input signal to the diode detector contains an ___ component and an ___ component.
During each carrier cycle, the diode turns on briefly and charges the capacitor in the detection circuit to the ___ voltage.
For effective demodulation, the time constant $RC$ should be ___ than the period of the carrier wave.
The demodulated af signal is amplified and converted back into sound by a ___.
